examples.question: A is B's brother. C is A's father. D is C's sister. B to D?
examples.solution:
Step 1: C is father of A & B.
Step 2: D is aunt of A & B.
Step 3: B is niece/nephew of D.

sections.concept: A+B means A is father of B.
sections.formula: Decode back to front or front to back.

examples.question: P+Q means P is father of Q. P-Q means P is sister. A+B-C. A to C?
examples.solution:
Step 1: B is sister of C.
Step 2: A is father of B.
Step 3: A is father of C.

shortcuts: Only son of my grandfather's only child = Me (if male).
commonMistakes: Assuming gender based on name (don't assume 'Ram' is male unless specified).
placementTips: Draw trees. Don't solve in head.
Start with 'My...' statements and work outwards.
